_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 FL state legislators (Franklin HD-8, Wright SD-8, Tant HD-9, McClain SD-9, Brannan HD-10). Currency fixes: Wright & Brannan term-limited / NOT running 2026; McClain not up until 2028. Cross-party catch: Franklin (D) voted YEA on HB1 universal vouchers (1 of 4 House Dems) and walked back agreeing with DeSantis on banning trans care for minors. Premise correction: McClain was a COSPONSOR (not prime sponsor) of the 2023 6-week ban; Brannan was a NON-VOTE on SB300 (not credited) and is NOT an HB931 sponsor (not credited).

gallop-franklin-ii@FL.notes_set: Democrat, FL House District 8 (Gadsden + part of Leon). Pharmacist (PharmD, FAMU); homeschooled through high school; House Democratic Leader-designate for 2028-2030. Running for reelection 2026 (Democratic primary canceled — unopposed; faces an Independent in November). Generally liberal (Freedom Index 0% in 2023) with TWO documented cross-party deviations. HARD: voted NAY on SB300 (2023 six-week ban); endorsed by the Florida Planned Parenthood PAC; sponsored a 2024 contraception-access bill. CROSS-PARTY (high value): voted YEA on HB1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers as one of only FOUR House Democrats, defending it on the record ('the future of student success depends on providing options'). SECOND deviation: told public radio he 'largely agrees' with DeSantis on banning puberty blockers/hormones/surgeries for transgender minors, then walked it back, apologized, and now backs access to 'comprehensive medical treatment'; endorsed by Equality Florida PAC (2026). Religion: no evidence found. HB931 (chaplains), HB543 (carry), SB1718 (immigration) individual votes not isolated — left null.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB300 (six-week ban); Florida Planned Parenthood PAC endorsee — affirmatively pro-abortion-rights, contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Voted against the abortion ban and sponsored a 2024 contraception-access bill — opposite direction from abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by the Florida Planned Parenthood PAC — abortion-industry alignment.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition.

1.note: No documented vote/statement on same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: Retracted his agreement with DeSantis on banning gender-transition treatments for minors; now backs access to such care; Equality Florida PAC endorsee — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Equality Florida PAC endorsement — supports LGBTQ-identity policy, contrary to opposing its promotion.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY: voted YEA on HB1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers as 1 of only 4 House Democrats, and defended it on the record.

1.note: No clear minor-medical-consent position (trans-care remark was retracted).

0.note: No evidence.
No evidence on CBDC/sound money.
No evidence on hand-counted paper ballots.

1.note: No evidence on photo voter ID.

4.note: No evidence on private election funding.

0.note: No affirmative pro-police action documented.

tom-a-wright@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL Senate District 8 (Volusia/New Smyrna Beach). Retired businessman; first elected 2018 (old District 14), redistricted to District 8 in 2022. CURRENCY FIX: TERM-LIMITED — NOT on the 2026 ballot (term ends Nov 2026; an open-seat GOP primary already underway to succeed him). Clean, consistent hard-conservative record with ZERO cross-party deviations across 10 verified Senate roll-call PDFs. HARD (flsenate.gov vote PDFs): YEA on SB300 (6-week ban); YEA on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education); YEA on SB254 (trans-care ban for minors); YEA on HB1 (universal vouchers); YEA on HB931 (school chaplains); YEA on SB7054 (anti-CBDC); YEA on SB7050 (2023 elections omnibus); YEA on SB450 (death penalty 8-4 jury); YEA on SB1718 (E-Verify/immigration); YEA on HB543 (constitutional carry). Religion: no evidence found.

allison-tant@FL.notes_set: Democrat, FL House District 9 (Tallahassee/Leon). Former chair of the Florida Democratic Party (2014-16); first seated Nov 2020; re-elected 2022 and 2024. Running for reelection 2026 (incumbent). Consistent party-line Democrat — Freedom Index 13% cumulative; NO cross-party deviation. EIGHT HARD NAY votes confirmed directly from myfloridahouse.gov roll-call PDFs ('N Tant-9' on each): SB300 (6-week ban) and HB5 (15-week ban) — also gave a floor speech against SB300; HB1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay'); HB1 (universal vouchers); HB931 (school chaplains); SB7050 (elections omnibus); SB1718 (immigration/E-Verify); HB543 (permitless carry). Endorsed by Equality Florida PAC (2026). Distinctive: disability advocacy (son with special needs) produced bipartisan bills (HB73, HB341) — not conservative-coded. Religion: no evidence found.

stan-mcclain@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL Senate District 9 (Marion/Levy/part of Alachua). State-certified building contractor; Marion County Commissioner 2004-2016; FL House 2016-2024; elected to Senate Nov 2024. CURRENCY FIX: NOT up in 2026 — Senate term runs to Nov 2028. PREMISE CORRECTION: McClain was a COSPONSOR (not the prime sponsor) of the 2023 six-week ban (HB7/companion SB300); the prime House sponsor was Jenna Persons-Mulicka. Consistent conservative; no cross-party deviation. HARD: cosponsor HB7 (2023 6-week ban); Florida Family Action Legislative Report Card A+ (100%, 2017-18); cosponsor HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education); YEA on HB1475 (2021 Fairness in Women's Sports); PRIME SPONSOR of HB931 (2024 school chaplains) — his marquee bill; cosponsor HB1 (2021 anti-rioting 'Combating Public Disorder'); cosponsor HB543 (2023 constitutional carry); cosponsor HB6003 (2020) to repeal the MSD Act red-flag/confiscation provisions; NRA-endorsed (2022). Economic/election/immigration individual votes not isolated — left null.

chuck-brannan-iii@FL.notes_set: Republican, FL House District 10 (Baker/Bradford/Columbia/Union + part of Alachua). Retired Chief Investigator, Baker County Sheriff's Office; former Deputy U.S. Marshal (29 yrs law enforcement); cattleman. First elected 2018; Chair of the House Judiciary Committee (2024-26). CURRENCY FIX: TERM-LIMITED — NOT running in 2026 (this is his final term; his son Chase Brannan is a separate 2026 HD-10 candidate). Uniformly conservative; no cross-party deviation. HARD: SPONSORED + voted YEA on HB5 (2022 15-week ban); voted YEA on HB1221 (2021 ban on genetic-disability abortions) and H289 (2026 fetal personhood); Florida Right to Life PAC endorsed (2022); ACU-FL 96%. NOTE: a NON-VOTE (absent) on SB300 (2023 6-week ban) — NOT credited as a yea. Voted YEA on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education); cosponsor of HB1 (2021 anti-rioting). PRIME SPONSOR of HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry) — his marquee bill; NRA 'A' + PVF endorsement (2022). CORRECTION: NOT a sponsor/cosponsor of HB931 (2024 chaplains) — not credited there.
